* Fix keyboard shortcuts in fullscreen with document-level capture listener

Add a document-level capture-phase keydown listener that only fires when
document.fullscreenElement is our video. In native fullscreen, events may
not propagate through the video element's regular event path, but they
always propagate through the document during capture phase. The guard
check (fullscreenElement === video && !defaultPrevented) is O(1) so adds
negligible overhead.

* Fix help-element blur: use clip-path spotlight on backdrop (#945)

* Initial plan

* Fix help-element blur: use clip-path spotlight on backdrop instead of z-index

The backdrop-filter: blur(4px) on the CDK overlay backdrop blurs everything
behind it. Since the help element lives in the regular DOM (inside app-root)
while the backdrop is in the CDK overlay container (a separate stacking
context), z-index alone cannot position the help element above the backdrop.

Fix: Apply a clip-path with an evenodd polygon to the backdrop element,
cutting a rectangular hole over the help element. This keeps the blur effect
on everything else while leaving the help element crisp and unblurred,
creating a spotlight effect.

commit.message:
Revert DLL loading changes and streamline command handling for BC28 (#222)

This pull request introduces a new approach for handling PowerShell
command overrides for Business Central version 28 and above, while
maintaining the existing method for earlier versions. The main change is
the addition of a dynamic wrapper function generator
(`Invoke-PwshOverwriting`) that creates PowerShell Core wrappers for
specified commands, improving compatibility and parameter handling.
Several minor code style and consistency improvements are also included.

**Dynamic command overriding for BC 28+:**

* Added `Invoke-PwshOverwriting` function in
`Export-PwshCoreOverride.ps1` that dynamically generates wrapper
functions for specified command names, enabling better parameter
handling and serialization for PowerShell Core in Business Central
version 28 and above.
* Updated `NavAppManagement.ps1` to use `Invoke-PwshOverwriting` for BC

commit.message:
fix: pre-load FFmpeg DLLs via LoadLibraryExW with absolute path

SetDllDirectoryW modifies the search order but the delay-load helper
can ignore it depending on how LoadLibraryEx is called internally.

New approach: LoadLibraryExW with the FULL absolute path to each DLL.
Windows caches modules by name, so when delay-load later calls
LoadLibrary('avcodec-61.dll') it finds the already-loaded module.
This is guaranteed to work regardless of search-path configuration.

Also use eprintln! before the logger is up so errors are always visible.

* Rename *Scale() to *Distribution() for unified naming

Unify the naming convention across SDK, CLI, and web editor:
- SDK: .lightnessScale() → .lightnessDistribution() (same for saturation/hue)
- CLI: --lightness-scale → --lightness-distribution (same for saturation/hue)
- Entity: lightnessScaleType → lightnessDistributionType (same for saturation/hue)

This aligns the ramp builder API with the color space .distribution()
method, which already used the correct naming. Both accept the same
ScaleType values (linear, fibonacci, ease-in, etc.).

Updated all tests, READMEs, SKILL.md, and export functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix mid-rotation face color flicker in hero cubes

The fragment shader was determining face color by classifying the
rotated normal against world axes. During diagonal tumbles, normals
cross axis boundaries causing faces to momentarily pick the wrong
color uniform.

Fix: bake face index (0-5) as a vertex attribute (aFI) so each
geometric face keeps its assigned color regardless of orientation.
Normals are still used for diffuse lighting, just not for color
selection.

commit.message:
fix: enable mouse support so scroll wheel scrolls terminal buffer

Add `set -g mouse on` so the mouse wheel enters copy-mode and scrolls
the terminal output buffer instead of sending up/down key events to the
shell (which navigated command history).

Also add a WheelUpPane binding that transparently passes scroll events
through when already in copy-mode or an alternate-screen application
(e.g. vim, less), and enters copy-mode for plain panes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add health check package for standardized service health endpoints (#14)

Provides:

Usage:

    health.NewChecker("db", func(ctx context.Context) error {
      return pool.Ping(ctx)

Auto-create lifecycle trip updates on status transitions, add multi-day trip tracking, toggle-day endpoint, and status transition validation (#79)

* Initial plan

* feat: auto-create lifecycle trip updates on status transitions and add multi-day trip tracking

- Create TripDay entity for tracking individual days in multi-day trips
- Add currentDay field to TripDetails for tracking current day number
- Make TripUpdate.location nullable for system-generated lifecycle updates
- Auto-create TRIP_STARTED update on CREATED → IN_PROGRESS transition
- Auto-create TRIP_ENDED update on * → FINISHED transition
- Auto-create DAY_END update on IN_PROGRESS → RESTING transition
- Auto-create DAY_START update on RESTING → IN_PROGRESS transition
- Manage TripDay records: open day 1 on trip start, close day on rest,
  open new day on resume, close open day on finish
- Add Liquibase migrations 028-030 for trip_days table, current_day column,
  and nullable trip_update location
- Add TripDayDTO, TripDayMapper, TripDayRepository
- Update TripDTO and TripDetailsDTO to include new fields
- Add comprehensive unit tests (23 tests, all passing)

Co-authored-by: tomassirio <19593836+tomassirio@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add PATCH /{id}/toggle-day endpoint for multi-day trip day toggling

- New PATCH endpoint toggles day state for MULTI_DAY trips
- IN_PROGRESS → RESTING: finishes current day (DAY_END update + TripDay close)
- RESTING → IN_PROGRESS: starts next day (DAY_START update + new TripDay)
- Rejects SIMPLE trips with IllegalStateException
- Rejects invalid statuses (CREATED, PAUSED, FINISHED)
- Validates active trip constraint when resuming from RESTING
- 8 new unit tests covering all scenarios

Co-authored-by: tomassirio <19593836+tomassirio@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add status transition validation matrix and block RESTING for non-MULTI_DAY trips

- TripStatus.canTransitionTo() enforces allowed transition matrix
- changeStatus() rejects invalid transitions before publishing events
- RESTING status restricted to MULTI_DAY trips only
- 10 unit tests for TripStatus transition matrix
- 7 unit tests for changeStatus() validation scenarios

Co-authored-by: tomassirio <19593836+tomassirio@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Refactor trip day management and introduce TripDayService for multi-day trip handling

fix: Suppress CodeQL false positives in test code

Add CodeQL suppression comments (lgtm[rust/hardcoded-credentials])
for hardcoded test credentials in auth.rs test module.

- Values are dummy test data, not real credentials
- Never used in production code

Fixed alerts:
- Line 187: test_auth_entry_to_netrc - "token123"
- Line 213: test_auth_entry_roundtrip - "pass"
- Line 225: test_parse_subscription_url - URL with embedded creds

Verified: All 74 unit tests still pass.

feat: optimize ruleRemoveById range handling store ranges instead of expanding to int slices (#1538)

* Initial plan

* Optimize ruleRemoveById range handling to avoid generating massive int slices

- Replace rangeToInts (which allocated []int of all matching rule IDs) with
  parseRange and parseIDOrRange helpers that return start/end integers
- For ctlRuleRemoveByID with ranges: store the range in
  Transaction.ruleRemoveByIDRanges ([][2]int) and check it in the rule
  evaluation loop, avoiding both the intermediate []int and potentially
  large map expansions
- For ctlRuleRemoveTargetByID: iterate rules once directly, eliminating
  the intermediate []int allocation
- Add RemoveRuleByIDRange method to Transaction
- Reset ruleRemoveByIDRanges on transaction pool reuse
- Replace TestCtlParseRange with TestCtlParseIDOrRange to test the new helpers

Co-authored-by: fzipi <3012076+fzipi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve test coverage for range-based rule removal

- Add TestRemoveRuleByIDRange in transaction_test.go:
  - range is stored in ruleRemoveByIDRanges
  - rules in range are skipped during Eval
  - multiple ranges work correctly
  - ruleRemoveByIDRanges is reset on transaction pool reuse
- Add TestCtlParseRange in ctl_test.go to cover parseRange directly
  (including the no-separator and start>end error paths)
- Add GetRuleRemoveByIDRanges() accessor on Transaction for cross-package
  test assertions
- Enhance "ruleRemoveById range" TestCtl case to verify the range is stored
- Add "ruleRemoveTargetById range" TestCtl case to verify range path works

Coverage changes:
  parseRange:         83.3% → 100%
  parseIDOrRange:     100%  (unchanged)
  RemoveRuleByIDRange: 0%   → 100%

Co-authored-by: fzipi <3012076+fzipi@users.noreply.github.com>
